title: Judge in Karen Read wrongful death suit probes leak of Proctor’s ‘sensitive’ information
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge Mark Gildea investigates a potential leak of private medical information in Karen Read's wrongful death lawsuit.
Sensitive data on Michael Proctor, a former State Police trooper, was allegedly leaked online shortly after being submitted.
Gildea pauses discovery from Proctor and warns of potential penalties for confidentiality breaches in the legal proceedings.

title: These are the beaches closed in Mass. as the heat wave hits
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Sewer main breaks in Haverhill led to untreated wastewater spilling into the Merrimack River, causing beach closures.
Massachusetts health officials monitor bacteria levels across over 1,100 beaches, with closures updated hourly on a state dashboard.
Closed beaches include all locations on Plum Island, Crystal Lake in Newton, and Johnson Street in Provincetown.

title: 17 books to read for America’s 250th anniversary
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local experts and booksellers curate 17 essential books for America's 250th anniversary.
The list includes new releases and classics addressing the nation's history and challenges.
Boston.com's staff collaborated with three Greater Boston independent bookstores for selections.

title: Brockton to implement curfew in response to shootings after World Cup matches
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Brockton will enforce a curfew from Friday 10 p.m. to Saturday 5 a.m. due to post-World Cup match shootings.
No entry will be allowed at bars after 7 p.m. Friday, and alcohol sales end at 9:30 p.m.
The city requested National Guard support citing public safety concerns after Cape Verdean World Cup celebrations turned violent.

title: Bruins bring back Connor Clifton on 2-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins signed defenseman Connor Clifton to a two-year contract with a $2.25 million annual cap hit.
Clifton returns to the Bruins, where he played 232 games over five seasons before joining the Sabres and Penguins.
Clifton's signing may indicate the end of Andrew Peeke's tenure, as Peeke enters the free-agent market.

title: 4 things to know about Mike Conley Jr. after reported one-year deal agreement with the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Mike Conley Jr., 38, has agreed to a one-year deal with the Boston Celtics.
Conley is a four-time NBA Sportsmanship Award winner, the most in the award's history.
Conley was represented by his father, an Olympic gold medalist and former NBA player agent.

title: Alec Baldwin – at Tanglewood this weekend – talks ’30 Rock,’ Boston accents, and New England movies
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Alec Baldwin to narrate Boston Symphony Orchestra's 'Lincoln Portrait' at Tanglewood on July 5.
Baldwin, a part-time Vermont resident, shares ties to New England through family and personal interests.
Baldwin discusses his career, love for classical music, and experiences with Boston accents.

title: Bruins trade Joonas Korpisalo to Rangers in a cap-clearing move
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded Joonas Korpisalo to the New York Rangers, clearing $3 million in cap space annually for two years.
In return, the Bruins received prospect Kalle Vaisanen and a 2028 fourth-round draft pick from New York.
Michael DiPietro, AHL MVP, is now favored to become the backup goalie, offering a cheaper alternative with an $812,500 cap hit.

title: State commission disqualifies GOP candidates for AG, Lt. Gov. after signature fraud allegations
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The State Ballot Law Commission disqualified GOP candidates Anne Manning Martin and Michael Walsh over signature fraud allegations.
Martin and Walsh's campaigns fell short of the required 10,000 valid signatures after over 1,000 were invalidated.
Both candidates have appealed the rulings; signature fraud involved allegedly forged signatures by consultant Joe Bronske.

title: Cambridge scraps its most controversial proposed alcohol rule changes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cambridge License Commission dropped plans for a one-hour earlier last call and a 30-minute wait between drink orders.
Public backlash led to the removal of restrictive alcohol proposals after feedback from residents and business owners.
Three listening sessions and formal discussions are planned before any rule changes are voted on in August and September.
